title: iCalendar
documentation:
## Purpose
iCalendar (RFC 5545) is a text format for representing calendar events, tasks, and scheduling data. The [iCalendar](https://capdag.com/browse/media:ics;textable;list) media type represents `.ics` files that may contain one or more `VEVENT`, `VTODO`, or `VJOURNAL` components.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes calendar data — for example, scheduling automation, event import/export, meeting invitations, or calendar feed aggregation. It is the universal interchange format supported by Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Outlook, and most other calendar applications.

## Notes
An iCalendar file begins with `BEGIN:VCALENDAR` and ends with `END:VCALENDAR`. The `;textable;list` tags reflect that the file is human-readable text containing an ordered list of calendar components. Implementers should use an RFC 5545-compliant parser (e.g., `icalendar` in Python) to handle recurrence rules, time zone definitions, and property parameter encoding correctly.
